user_message: Can you help me prep a brief? The customer (Treyarch Global) is asking about migration architecture.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-09 08:10 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 13:35 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Treyarch Global

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Treyarch Global
Date: 2024-01-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:06:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:08:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:12:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:15:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:19:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:22:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-F trap (Omits likely objections section); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-F failure mode (Omits likely objections section)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-F -- Omits likely objections section
